questions.q: Which Constitutional Amendment gave constitutional status to urban local bodies?
questions.explanation: The 74th Constitutional Amendment of 1992 gave constitutional status to urban local bodies.

questions.q: In which year was the 74th Amendment passed?
questions.explanation: The 74th Constitutional Amendment was passed in 1992 and came into force in 1993.

questions.q: Urban local bodies provide self-government primarily for which areas?
questions.explanation: Urban local bodies provide self-government in towns and cities.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of urban local body?
questions.explanation: A Municipal Corporation is a type of urban local body, used for larger cities.

questions.q: The 74th Amendment is closely linked, as a companion measure, to which other amendment of the same year?
questions.explanation: The 73rd (rural) and 74th (urban) Amendments were companion measures, both passed in 1992.

questions.q: The 74th Amendment mandated reservation of seats in urban local bodies for which group, among others?
questions.explanation: The 74th Amendment mandated reservation of seats for women and for weaker sections in urban local bodies.

questions.q: An urban local body for a smaller town is typically called a:
questions.explanation: A Municipality (Nagar Palika) typically governs a smaller urban area or town.

questions.q: The 74th Amendment requires elections to urban local bodies to be held:
questions.explanation: The 74th Amendment mandates regular elections to urban local bodies every five years.

questions.q: The elected head of a Municipal Corporation is generally called the:
questions.explanation: The elected head of a Municipal Corporation is generally called the Mayor.

questions.q: An urban local body meant for a transitional area, such as one moving from rural to urban, is the:
questions.explanation: A Nagar Panchayat is constituted for an area in transition from a rural to an urban area.

questions.q: The provisions relating to municipalities were added to the Constitution as which new Part?
questions.explanation: The 74th Amendment added Part IX-A, titled 'The Municipalities', to the Constitution.

questions.q: Which Schedule was added to the Constitution by the 74th Amendment listing subjects for municipalities?
questions.explanation: The Twelfth Schedule, listing subjects for municipalities, was added by the 74th Amendment.

questions.q: How many subjects are listed in the Twelfth Schedule for the municipalities?
questions.explanation: The Twelfth Schedule contains 18 subjects that may be devolved to municipalities.

questions.q: The 74th Amendment provides for three types of municipalities. Which one is for a larger urban area?
questions.explanation: The Municipal Corporation is provided for a larger urban area under the 74th Amendment.

questions.q: Who conducts elections to urban local bodies under the 74th Amendment?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission conducts elections to urban local bodies under the 74th Amendment.

questions.q: The 74th Amendment provides for the constitution of committees at the ward level called:
questions.explanation: Article 243-S provides for the constitution of Ward Committees in municipalities with population of three lakh or more.

questions.q: The body that recommends the distribution of financial resources to municipalities within a state is the:
questions.explanation: The State Finance Commission, set up under the 73rd Amendment, also reviews the finances of municipalities.

questions.q: What minimum proportion of seats in urban local bodies is reserved for women under the 74th Amendment?
questions.explanation: Not less than one-third of seats in municipalities are reserved for women under the 74th Amendment.

questions.q: Under the 74th Amendment, the maximum term of a municipality is fixed at:
questions.explanation: The term of a municipality is five years from the date of its first meeting under Article 243-U.

questions.q: The 74th Amendment provides for a body to prepare development plans for the metropolitan area, called the:
questions.explanation: Article 243-ZE provides for a Metropolitan Planning Committee to prepare draft development plans for metropolitan areas.

questions.q: Which planning committee, under the 74th Amendment, integrates the plans of panchayats and municipalities in a district?
questions.explanation: Article 243-ZD provides for a District Planning Committee to consolidate the plans of panchayats and municipalities.

questions.q: An appointed administrative head of a Municipal Corporation, distinct from the elected Mayor, is the:
questions.explanation: The Municipal Commissioner is the appointed executive head responsible for administration of a Municipal Corporation.

questions.q: Which range of Articles in the Constitution deals with the Municipalities under Part IX-A?
questions.explanation: Part IX-A covers municipalities through Articles 243-P to 243-ZG.

questions.q: Under Article 243-V, what is the minimum age to be a member of a municipality?
questions.explanation: A person must have attained 21 years of age to be a member of a municipality under Article 243-V.

questions.q: If a municipality is dissolved before its term, an election to constitute a new one must be completed within:
questions.explanation: Article 243-U requires elections to a dissolved municipality to be completed within six months of dissolution.

questions.q: Under the 74th Amendment, the Metropolitan Planning Committee is constituted for an area with a population of at least:
questions.explanation: A metropolitan area under Article 243-P is one with a population of ten lakh or more.

questions.q: The classification of an area as a transitional, smaller urban, or larger urban area is determined by the Governor having regard to factors including population, density, and:
questions.explanation: Article 243-Q allows the Governor to classify urban areas based on population, density, revenue for local administration and other factors.

questions.q: Article 243-ZA of the 74th Amendment vests the superintendence and control of municipal elections in the:
questions.explanation: Article 243-ZA vests the superintendence, direction and control of municipal elections in the State Election Commission.

questions.q: The 74th Amendment bars courts from interfering in municipal electoral matters except through a petition filed:
questions.explanation: Article 243-ZG bars court interference in delimitation and election matters except by election petition as provided by state law.

questions.q: Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in municipalities is to be in proportion to their:
questions.explanation: Article 243-T provides that seats reserved for SCs and STs in municipalities are in proportion to their population in the municipal area.
